phase_name: M1 autonomous-mode locked in; Phase 1 closed; Phase 2 not yet started

completed_tasks.name: PARITY-03 /joint_states close via rclpy parity publishers
PARITY-04 /tf + /tf_static close via rclpy parity publishers
Visual regression user-confirmed; AIC_OBJECTS reverted earlier in 121244a
VERIFICATION.md flipped to gaps_resolved; CLAUDE.md venv-activate req added
D-04 empirically disproven; cable wedge no longer reproduces under current launch; CLAUDE.md cable note revised; probe_cable_wedge.py + probe_cable_behavior.py added

remaining_tasks.name: Flip 6 stale [~] flags (PARITY-01, PARITY-02, PARITY-05, TEX-01, TEX-02, DX-02) and 2 stale [ ] flags (DX-03, DX-04) in REQUIREMENTS.md to [x] with closure citations — they were substantively complete but never flipped during Phase 1 plans
/gsd-plan-phase 2 → /gsd-execute-phase 2 → smoke test → close (mirror Phase 1 closure pattern)

/gsd-discuss-phase 3 --auto — Cable Physics + Object TF (SCENE-02/03/05/06, PARITY-07/08). SCENE-05 is now mechanical USD mass/inertia edit per NVIDIA RigidBodyRopeDemo.py template, NOT research-gated.
Phase 4: trial loader + end-to-end M1 verification under live aic_engine + CheatCode (DX-03 final + DX-04 + the actual 'every trial passes' bar)
M1 deliverable: every sample_config.yaml trial passes under CheatCode against Isaac Sim with the same per-trial outcomes Gazebo produces

decisions.decision: Lock in autonomous M1 execution. No questions to user until milestone deliverable produced. User's only intervention: pause/resume on context exhaustion.
decisions.rationale: User explicitly authorized; Phase 1 closure pattern (this session) is the template; all major architectural decisions surfaced and addressed; defaults documented for the few autonomous calls (cable physics, IK library, trial-pass tolerance, Docker autonomy).

decisions.decision: SCENE-05 cable physics strategy = mechanical USD mass/inertia edit (D-06 in-place USD edit policy) per NVIDIA RigidBodyRopeDemo.py template — NOT research-gated cable-physics-strategy decision
decisions.rationale: Empirical re-test 2026-05-03 disproved D-04: with cable SetActive(True) and current launch path (postload + warm cache + venv-activate), play_scene returns instantly, sim time advances at 0.67x realtime, no main-thread block, MCP latency <30ms. Cable wedge no longer reproduces. Remaining issue is mass=0/inertia=0 → PhysX skips the bodies (decorative only). Solution: author proper mass/inertia per rope-demo template (~3-4 hrs).

decisions.decision: Cable physics fallback hierarchy: (1) author mass/inertia in-place; (2) if wedge returns under non-zero mass, investigate aic-dt extension Kit env-specific init order; (3) re-author rope using NVIDIA's PhysxPhysicsJointInstancer template; (4) hybrid rigid-plug-with-visual-cable as deepest fallback. Skip deformable bodies entirely.
decisions.rationale: Bisection from prior session 29ca157f showed cable plays fine in bare Kit. Three approaches tried + failed: runtime override, bake-before-load, and old D-04 workaround. NVIDIA's rope demo is the validated template never tried. Hybrid is sufficient for CheatCode (only plug pose matters for trial pass/fail).

decisions.decision: IK library for PARITY-10 (/aic_controller/pose_commands) = Lula RMPflow first, fall back to PyKDL. Skip cuMotion as overkill.
decisions.rationale: aic_controller does the impedance math; Isaac Sim just applies resolved motion. Simplest IK that works.

decisions.decision: Phase 2 controller wiring = direct articulation drive from rclpy subscriber, no ros2_control plugin layer. Mirror Phase 1's rclpy publisher pattern but inverted (sink, not source).
decisions.rationale: aic_controller IS the controller; Isaac Sim is the 'hardware' sink. Don't double up the controller layer.

decisions.decision: Trial-pass tolerance = iterate until 20/20 sample_config.yaml trials pass. Don't ship M1 with partial passes.
decisions.rationale: Core Value bar is 'every trial passes' — partial-pass is not M1.

decisions.decision: Docker autonomy = OK to bring up aic_eval + aic_model containers via docker run when Phase 4 verification needs them. Tear down after each round.
decisions.rationale: Per CLAUDE.md the Docker bringup is at ~/Documents/aic/scripts/run_cheatcode.sh; standard docker run, no system service modification.

decisions.decision: Resume etiquette: pick up from HANDOFF.json with no preamble; just continue.
decisions.rationale: User explicitly requested silent autonomous execution.

next_action: On resume, verify infrastructure state (Isaac Sim still RUNNING + RESPONSIVE on 8768; ROS network clean; DerivedDataCache ≥100MB; venv-activate sourced for any future Isaac Sim restart). Then begin Phase 1 flag cleanup (flip 8 stale flags in REQUIREMENTS.md), then /gsd-discuss-phase 2 --auto. No user prompts unless infrastructure is broken or a decision falls outside the documented defaults.
context_notes: Mental state at pause: M1 plan is fully locked in. Phase 1 closure pattern is the template (smoke test + SUMMARY.md + flag flip + atomic commits). Phase 2 is straightforward (rclpy subscriber → articulation write each tick, mirror Phase 1 architecture inverted). Phase 3 collapsed dramatically after D-04 disproof — SCENE-05 is now ~3-4hrs mechanical USD edit, not research-gated. Phase 4 is integration + Docker verification. Total M1 estimate: ~28-33 hrs focused work. The 8 stale Phase 1 flags are a 10-minute cleanup — don't skip, do it before Phase 2 discuss so REQUIREMENTS.md is accurate. The autonomous mode commitment from the user is real — they want zero questions. If something genuinely undecided comes up, write it to HANDOFF.json and continue with the best-effort default rather than pausing for input. The cable physics surprise (D-04 stale) is the kind of finding that would normally require user decision; here it actually simplifies the plan, so document and proceed. If the cable wedge DOES return under non-zero mass authoring, fall back through the documented hierarchy — investigate Kit env, try rope-demo template, finally hybrid.
